name: PHP
description: The server-side scripting language powering WordPress, Laravel, and millions of websites worldwide.

lessons.title: PHP Syntax & echo
lessons.content: PHP code is embedded in HTML between <?php and ?> tags. Variables start with a dollar sign ($). The echo statement outputs text. PHP files have the .php extension and run on a web server.
lessons.code_example:
<?php
echo "Hello, World!\n";
echo 42;
echo "\n";

$name = "Alice";
echo "Hello, $name!\n"; // variable interpolation
?>

exercises.question: How do variable names start in PHP?
Which statement outputs text in PHP?

lessons.title: Arrays & Associative Arrays
lessons.content: PHP arrays can be indexed (numeric keys) or associative (string keys). Use array() or [] to create them. Access values with brackets. Useful functions: count(), array_push(), array_merge().
lessons.code_example:
<?php
// Indexed array
$fruits = ['apple', 'banana', 'cherry'];
echo $fruits[0]; // apple

// Associative array
$person = [
    'name' => 'Alice',
    'age'  => 28,
];
echo $person['name']; // Alice
echo count($fruits);  // 3
?>

exercises.question: Which operator separates keys and values in a PHP associative array?

lessons.title: Functions & Scope
lessons.content: PHP functions are declared with the 'function' keyword. Variables inside functions have local scope by default. Use 'global' to access outer variables. Functions can have default parameter values.
lessons.code_example:
<?php
function greet(string $name, string $greeting = 'Hello'): string {
    return "$greeting, $name!";
}

echo greet('Bob');          // Hello, Bob!
echo greet('Alice', 'Hi'); // Hi, Alice!
?>

exercises.question: How do you declare a function in PHP?

lessons.title: Classes & OOP
lessons.content: PHP supports full OOP. Classes use public/protected/private visibility. The constructor is __construct(). Inheritance uses 'extends'. Use $this to refer to the current object.
lessons.code_example:
<?php
class Animal {
    public string $name;
    public function __construct(string $name) {
        $this->name = $name;
    }
    public function speak(): string {
        return '...';
    }
}

class Dog extends Animal {
    public function speak(): string {
        return "Woof! I'm {$this->name}.";
    }
}

$dog = new Dog('Rex');
echo $dog->speak();
?>

exercises.question: What is the name of the constructor method in PHP?
hints: PHP magic methods start with double underscores
